entities.name: Ilse
entities.context: Walks through Careerspan's employer portal conceptually, explains product positioning

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: Company building employer assessment platform using story-based candidate evaluation

entities.name: Rich stories
entities.context: Candidates tell stories about work and life instead of submitting resumes

entities.name: Role decomposition
entities.context: Breaking roles into 10-12 responsibilities plus hard/soft skills with importance and competence levels

entities.name: Assessment criteria
entities.context: Defines what success looks like at various competence levels and how non-traditional backgrounds can evidence capability

entities.name: Competence levels
entities.context: Scale from basic to expert for evaluating skills

entities.name: Transferable evidence
entities.context: How non-traditional backgrounds can still demonstrate capability for a criterion

entities.name: Cultural fit
entities.context: Constraint area Careerspan currently does less inference around

entities.name: Hard blockers
entities.context: Must-have requirements like professional experience that are handled via explicit questions

entities.name: Employer portal
entities.context: Careerspan interface for employers to view candidate analysis

entities.name: Survey questions
entities.context: Method for handling cultural fit and hard blockers rather than inference

relationships.context: Presents and explains Careerspan's product positioning
Core input method replacing traditional resumes
Breaks down roles into structured criteria for assessment
Generates 60-90 pages of analysis tracking ~30 criteria per candidate
Each skill annotated with required competence level
Criteria track both direct and transferable evidence of capability
Handles cultural fit and hard blockers explicitly rather than via inference
Both handled as constraints outside main inference system
